claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) alone is insufficient for production agents; skills defining domain knowledge are needed for agents to follow environment-specific constraints
actionable: Pair MCP tool definitions with skill.md files that specify workflows, security rules, and domain expertise; don't rely on MCP alone
source_title: Combine Skills and MCP to Close the Context Gap — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Critical security information (like PostgreSQL RLS security_invoker flags) must be in skill.md directly, not reference files, because agents skip reference documents
actionable: Put non-negotiable security requirements directly in skill.md with clear explanations of why they matter, not in separate reference documents
source_title: Combine Skills and MCP to Close the Context Gap — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Point agent skills to live documentation (via SSH/remote file access) rather than duplicating documentation to avoid staleness
actionable: Expose your documentation via SSH or HTTP so agents can fetch current docs instead of embedding stale copies in skill files
source_title: Combine Skills and MCP to Close the Context Gap — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Opinionated workflow guidance in skills (specific steps agents should follow) matters more than comprehensive feature documentation
actionable: In skills.md, recommend specific step-by-step workflows rather than listing all possible features; agents follow guidance better than explore options
source_title: Combine Skills and MCP to Close the Context Gap — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase

claim: Agent evaluation with evals across Claude and GPT models in multiple test conditions (baseline, MCP-only, MCP+skills) shows skills add 15-25% performance lift
actionable: Run evals against your agent with and without skills to measure the guidance multiplier effect on task completion
source_title: Combine Skills and MCP to Close the Context Gap — Pedro Rodrigues, Supabase
